w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: AI-Integrated Management System with SMS Reminder for House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming Salon - Fairview
In Partial Fulfillment of the Requirements for
Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming Salon, located in Fairview 1, Quezon City, provides both veterinary and grooming services for pets in the local community. The clinic offers routine medical check-ups, vaccinations, laboratory examinations, prescription management, and grooming services, with the goal of ensuring the health and well-being of pets while offering convenience and high-quality service to their owners.

Currently, the clinic relies entirely on paper-based record keeping. All patient records are manually recorded using paper logbooks and forms, which include client and owner details, pet information, the purpose of the visit (such as check-up, vaccination, or grooming), and chief complaints such as vomiting or diarrhea. Prescriptions are written manually, and there is no automated system for linking these prescriptions to the client’s record. Likewise, reminders for vaccinations, follow-up visits, and laboratory check-ups are tracked manually by staff.

This manual system has caused several significant problems. A major issue occurred when a typhoon destroyed fifteen years’ worth of patient records, resulting in the permanent loss of important patient medical history and requiring the clinic to manually re-record all information. This event caused operational disruption and highlighted the vulnerability of physical record storage. Furthermore, because the clinic is required to retain client records for at least three years, the manual system makes records difficult to store, easy to damage or lose, and hard to retrieve when client history is needed.
The clinic also experiences workflow inefficiencies. Staff must manually track appointments and reminders,

their workload and creating the possibility of human error or missed schedules. Prescription management is similarly inefficient: manually generated prescriptions must be recorded and linked to client files, and retrieving past prescription history is time-consuming. Client records are not easily searchable, retrievable, or exportable for reporting or printing purposes.

Addressing these issues is important because the current system increases the risk of data loss, delays in patient care, missed follow-ups, and overall operational inefficiency. To solve these challenges, this study proposes the development of a web-based AI-Integrated Management System with SMS reminders. The system will digitize and securely store all patient records, automate reminders for vaccinations, follow-ups, and laboratory check-ups via SMS, generate and link prescriptions automatically, and make client records easily searchable, retrievable, and exportable. This system aims to prevent future data loss, reduce staff workload, improve workflow efficiency, and ensure continuity of care for pets.
Review of Related Literature, Studies, and Systems

House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming Salon currently relies on a fully paper-based system for recording and managing client and patient information. This manual process affects the clinic’s ability to securely store records, quickly retrieve patient history, manage prescriptions efficiently, and ensure timely follow-ups. As a result, clinic operations are slowed down, staff workload increases, and patient data remains vulnerable to loss or damage.
The clinic is unable to securely store, efficiently manage, and quickly retrieve patient records and reminders due to its reliance on a manual paper-based system, highlighting the need for an
AI-integrated management system with SMS reminders.
Permanent loss of patient records during disasters, such as typhoons
Difficulty in storing, searching, and retrieving paper-based client and pet records.
High staff workload caused by manually tracking appointments, vaccinations, follow-ups, and prescriptions.
Inefficient prescription management due to manual recording and lack of automatic linkage to client records.
Lack of automated reminders for vaccinations, follow-ups, and laboratory check-ups, affecting timely care for pets.

To develop a web-based AI-Integrated Management System with SMS reminders that will securely store, efficiently manage, and quickly retrieve patient records and reminders at House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming Salon – Fairview.
Prevent permanent data loss by implementing secure cloud-based storage with automatic backups and proper record retention.
Digitize and organize client records to allow fast searching, filtering, and retrieval of patient information.
Automate prescription generation and ensure prescriptions are automatically linked to client records and available for PDF export.
Automate Client Reminders by sending automated SMS notifications for vaccination due dates, follow-up visits, and laboratory examinations.
Reduce staff workload and improve service efficiency by minimizing manual recording and tracking tasks.
This study is anchored on the Information Systems Success Model developed by
and McLean (1992), which explains that the success and necessity of an information system are

by its System Quality, Information Quality, and Service Quality, which influence User Satisfaction and produce Net Benefits for an organization. The model explains why information systems are needed in organizations experiencing inefficiencies, data management issues, and operational challenges.

In the case of House of Puppies Veterinary Clinic & Grooming Salon, the reliance on a paper-based system has resulted in data loss, difficulty in retrieving patient records, inefficient prescription management, and increased staff workload. According to the Information Systems Success Model, improving System Quality through a secure, reliable, and cloud-based digital platform can prevent data loss and ensure system availability. Enhancing Information Quality by organizing and digitizing patient records improves accuracy, accessibility, and
retrieval of information. Service Quality is improved through a user-friendly interface and automated processes such as SMS reminders and prescription generation.

These improvements are expected to increase User Satisfaction among clinic staff and generate Net Benefits, including reduced workload, improved workflow efficiency, prevention of future data loss, secure record retention, and better continuity of care for pets. Thus, the model explains why the proposed AI-Integrated Management System is necessary and how its quality dimensions lead to organizational improvement.
Furthermore, this study is supported by the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM), which explains how users come to accept and use
. TAM states that Perceived Usefulness and Perceived Ease of Use influence a user’s Behavioral Intention to Use a system, which then leads to Actual System Use.

In this study, Perceived Usefulness refers to the extent to which clinic staff believe that the proposed system will improve efficiency, reduce manual workload, automate reminders, and simplify record retrieval. Perceived Ease of Use refers to how easy the system is to learn, navigate, and
If the staff perceive the system as useful and easy to use, they are more likely to accept and utilize it in their daily operations.
By integrating the Information Systems Success Model and the Technology Acceptance Model, this study ensures that the proposed system is not only technically effective but also accepted and
by its intended users. The Information Systems Success Model explains how the system improves organizational performance, while the Technology Acceptance Model explains how user
influence successful system adoption. Together, these theories provide
for the development and implementation of the proposed web-based AI-Integrated Management System with SMS reminders.
Scope and Delimitation of the Study
